query:
You are working on the pinterest/ktlint repository.
The codebase is at version 0.49 (commit: f37fdc97).

## Issue to Resolve

False positive indent after updating from 0.47.1
## Expected Behavior

Following code should pass indent validation:
```kotlin
    private fun test(): Boolean? =
        runCatching { true }
            .getOrNull()?.let { result ->
                !result
            }
```

It's also how IDEA indents code.

## Observed Behavior

0.48.2 and current master ([44ad030](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/commit/44ad0303546f07887058ed444a73cd9c1df1e712)) return:

```
[LintError(line=4, col=1, ruleId=standard:indent, detail=Unexpected indentation (12) (should be 8), canBeAutoCorrected=true),
    LintError(line=5, col=1, ruleId=standard:indent, detail=Unexpected indentation (8) (should be 4), canBeAutoCorrected=true)]
```

This snippet was accepted by 0.47.1.

## Relevant Files

The following files are likely relevant to solving this issue:
- `CHANGELOG.md`
- `ktlint-ruleset-standard/src/main/kotlin/com/pinterest/ktlint/ruleset/standard/rules/IndentationRule.kt`

## Instructions

Please analyze this issue and implement a fix. Make the necessary code changes to resolve the problem described above.

Focus on:
- Understanding the root cause of the issue
- Making minimal, targeted changes to fix the problem
- Ensuring your changes don't break existing functionality

## Important Evaluation Notes

**Your work will be evaluated by running hidden tests that you do not have access to.** These tests will verify that your fix correctly addresses the issue. You should be confident in your solution before finishing.
**If you write tests to verify your changes, create them in NEW test files only** - do NOT modify existing test files

## CRITICAL RESTRICTIONS

**NEVER use git commands.** Do not run `git status`, `git diff`, `git checkout`, `git reset`, or ANY other git commands. Using git will corrupt the evaluation environment and invalidate your entire submission. All file operations should be done through normal file editing and reading tools, not git.

393→            fromAstNode = node,
394→            toAstNode = rbrace,
395→            firstChildIndent = "",
396→            lastChildIndent = "",
397→        )
398→
399→        // Inner indent context in reversed order
400→        node
401→            .treeParent
402→            ?.takeIf { it.elementType == FUNCTION_LITERAL }
403→            ?.findChildByType(ARROW)
404→            ?.let { arrow ->
405→                startIndentContext(

package com.pinterest.ktlint.ruleset.standard

import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.Rule
import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.RuleId
import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.editorconfig.EditorConfigProperty

internal val STANDARD_RULE_ABOUT = Rule.About(
    maintainer = "KtLint",
    repositoryUrl = "https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint",
    issueTrackerUrl = "https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues",
)

/**
 * Standard rules can only be declared and instantiated in the 'ktlint-ruleset-standard'. Custom rule set providers or API consumers have
 * to extend the [Rule] class to define a custom rule.
 */
public open class StandardRule internal constructor(
    id: String,
    override val visitorModifiers: Set<VisitorModifier> = emptySet(),
    override val usesEditorConfigProperties: Set<EditorConfigProperty<*>> = emptySet(),
) : Rule(
    ruleId = RuleId("$STANDARD_RULE_SET_ID:$id"),
    visitorModifiers = visitorModifiers,
    usesEditorConfigProperties = usesEditorConfigProperties,
    about = STANDARD_RULE_ABOUT,
)

content.text:
I have resolved the issue where mixing dot-qualified expressions and safe-access expressions in a chain caused false positive indentation errors in `IndentationRule`.

### Root Cause
The issue was caused by `IndentationRule` starting a new indentation context whenever a `DOT_QUALIFIED_EXPRESSION` was nested inside a `SAFE_ACCESS_EXPRESSION` (or vice versa). Since these are different element types, the existing logic treated them as distinct nesting levels, leading to multiple levels of indentation being applied to the same call chain.

### Fix
I have updated `IndentationRule.kt` to treat `DOT_QUALIFIED_EXPRESSION`, `SAFE_ACCESS_EXPRESSION`, and `USER_TYPE` as part of the same group of qualified expressions. The rule now checks if the parent node is also one of these types before starting a new indentation context. This ensures that a single level of indentation is maintained for the entire chain, regardless of whether it uses `.` or `?.`.

### Changes
-   Modified `ktlint-ruleset-standard/src/main/kotlin/com/pinterest/ktlint/ruleset/standard/rules/IndentationRule.kt`:
    -   Added a private constant `QUALIFIED_EXPRESSION_TYPES` containing `DOT_QUALIFIED_EXPRESSION`, `SAFE_ACCESS_EXPRESSION`, and `USER_TYPE`.
    -   Updated the `visit` logic for these types to skip starting a new indentation context if the parent node is already one of these types.

This change aligns `ktlint`'s behavior with IDEA's default indentation and restores the behavior from version 0.47.1.

Tested with the reported code snippet and other variations of nested call chains, ensuring they now pass without lint violations.

package com.pinterest.ktlint.ruleset.standard

import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.Rule
import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.RuleId
import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.editorconfig.EditorConfigProperty

internal val STANDARD_RULE_ABOUT = Rule.About(
    maintainer = "KtLint",
    repositoryUrl = "https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint",
    issueTrackerUrl = "https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues",
)

/**
 * Standard rules can only be declared and instantiated in the 'ktlint-ruleset-standard'. Custom rule set providers or API consumers have
 * to extend the [Rule] class to define a custom rule.
 */
public open class StandardRule internal constructor(
    id: String,
    override val visitorModifiers: Set<VisitorModifier> = emptySet(),
    override val usesEditorConfigProperties: Set<EditorConfigProperty<*>> = emptySet(),
) : Rule(
    ruleId = RuleId("$STANDARD_RULE_SET_ID:$id"),
    visitorModifiers = visitorModifiers,
    usesEditorConfigProperties = usesEditorConfigProperties,
    about = STANDARD_RULE_ABOUT,
)
